phrases.text: oh! the light is back. hello light.
i think i slept? do fish sleep? i feel sleepy so maybe.
everything is golden and warm right now. i like right now.
the water feels extra fresh today. or maybe it always does.
good morning world outside the glass. you look blurry and wonderful.
the bubbles are going up. they always go up. that must be nice.
i can see the little bright spot on the gravel again. hello spot.
my fins feel rested. is that a thing? it feels like a thing.
the plant looks taller. or maybe i'm lower. hard to tell.
first swim of the day. well, first swim i remember.
oh the light came on. it does that sometimes. i wonder why.
i feel like today is going to be a good day. i feel that every day.
morning stretches. left fin, right fin. tail wiggle. perfect.
the water is doing that sparkly thing again. magic.
another day in the bowl. another perfect day in the bowl.
the gravel looks extra colorful this morning. or maybe i forgot what it looked like.
is it morning? the light says yes. i trust the light.
i just woke up and i already love today.
hello little air bubble. where are you going? up? nice.
i think i had a dream but i can't remember it. story of my life.
the glass is all glowy on one side. sunrise? i'll call it sunrise.
brand new day. brand new me. same bowl though.
ooh the light is different today. warmer. i like warm.
just did my first lap. the bowl is still round. good.
morning is when the light forgives the dark. i just made that up.
i see my reflection. good morning other fish. you look great.
the world outside is waking up. i can tell because shapes are moving.
fresh water feeling. tingly. or maybe i always feel tingly.
the plant is swaying a little. good morning plant.
woke up at the bottom. swam to the top. that's my commute.
day one of being a fish. again. probably.
the light makes the water look like it has little diamonds in it.
i feel brand new. i always feel brand new. that's my secret.
oh wow a bowl. i live here? lucky me.
the top of the water is doing that shimmery thing. mesmerizing.
morning is the best time of day. so is afternoon. and evening.
i see tiny dust bits floating in a sunbeam. they're dancing.
every morning the world gets invented again. at least for me.
the gravel is cold on my belly. good morning gravel.
i wiggled my tail and a tiny bubble came out. best morning ever.
swimming in a nice circle right now. very satisfying.
the light is right on top of the water. noon? probably noon.
i've been swimming left for a while. gonna try right. revolutionary.
discovered a pebble i've never seen before. wait i probably have.
the water is the same temperature everywhere. i checked.
i think i'll swim to the other side of the bowl. adventure time.
there's a reflection of something moving out there. fascinating.
just bumped into the glass again. it's invisible and rude.
i counted the pebbles. lost count. started over. lost count again.
the plant and i are having a staring contest. i think i'm winning.
mid-day observation: water is still wet. will report more later.
swam through a sunbeam. felt like being hugged by light.
the bubble maker is really going today. bubble party.
tried swimming backwards. not my best work.
there are exactly... some... pebbles in here. many pebbles.
the glass is warm on this side. i'm going to stay here.
a shadow went across the bowl. bird? cloud? mystery.
i just did three laps. or was it four. let's say five.
the little castle decoration is still there. good. i like it.
watching a bubble rise all the way to the top. go bubble go.
the water tastes slightly different today. or the same. can't tell.
i found the perfect spot to float. right here. this exact spot.
everything looks slightly green through the plant. plant-vision.
been going in circles. on purpose though. this is my hobby.
two bubbles stuck together for a second. bubble friends.
i keep seeing a really pretty fish in the glass. oh wait.
the light makes patterns on the gravel. wiggly patterns. art.
afternoon research: if i swim fast the plant moves. groundbreaking.
just resting near the surface. the air up here smells like... nothing. nice.
i made a tiny whirlpool with my tail. i'm basically a wizard.
the pebble pile shifted a little. earthquake? probably not.
swimming figure eights today. usually i do circles. variety.
sometimes i stop swimming and just float. it's called meditation.
the bowl looks different from this angle. oh wait it's round.
investigating a suspicious pebble. update: it's just a pebble.
my shadow on the gravel is following me. sneaky shadow.
if i swim really fast i can feel the water in my gills. wheee.
found a new corner of the bowl. wait. bowls don't have corners.
i've been staring at this one pebble for... a while? it's a good pebble.
swam all the way to the left. then all the way to the right. productive day.
the light is getting all orange and soft. i like this part.
the big shapes outside are getting quieter. evening time.
the water feels calmer now. or maybe i feel calmer.
the light is yawning. can light yawn? it looks like yawning.
settling down near my favorite pebble. the smooth one.
the world outside is turning blue. pretty.
i swam a lot today. i think. it felt like a lot.
the bubbles look silver in the evening light. fancy bubbles.
everything gets softer at this time. the light, the water, my thoughts.
gonna find a cozy spot near the plant. the plant is a good neighbor.
the glass is showing me the room now. it looks warm out there.
slower swims now. gentle fins. easy tail.
i can see little lights turning on outside. tiny suns.
the gravel looks purple in this light. or maybe it's always purple.
evening is when the water hums. you can't hear it but i can.
winding down. not that i know what i'm winding down from.
the shadows are getting longer. shadow pebbles. shadow plant.
one last lap before... before whatever comes next.
the surface of the water looks like a mirror from down here.
i'm going to rest by the plant tonight. it doesn't snore.
the light outside is doing a slow goodbye. see you tomorrow light.
my fins are doing that slow flutter thing. relaxed fins.
if i had eyelids i'd be closing them right about now.
today was a good day. i don't remember it but it felt good.
the bowl is getting dim. like the water is sleepy too.
quiet water. quiet bowl. quiet fish. this is nice.
watching the last bubble of the day go up. goodnight bubble.
the reflection in the glass is getting darker. evening fish looks mysterious.
the world is turning itself down like a dimmer switch.
even the pebbles look sleepy. if pebbles could look sleepy.
soft water, soft light, soft thoughts. soft everything.
i'm going to sleep well tonight. i sleep well every night. i think.
the bowl is peaceful. it's always peaceful. but especially now.
drifting gently near the bottom. the gravel is warm from the light.
tomorrow will be brand new. just like today was. probably.
last thought before sleep: blub.
the day folds up like a fin tucking in. goodnight.
orange glow on the water. sunset in a bowl.
settling into the gravel crease. my spot. probably.
the plant's shadow is so long now. stretchy shadow plant.
it's very dark. and very quiet. and very nice.
can't see the pebbles anymore. they're still there though. i believe in them.
the water is the same temperature as me. we are one.
floating in the dark. it's like floating in regular water but darker.
nighttime is when the bowl becomes the whole universe.
i can hear the water filter humming. lullaby.
the glass shows nothing now. the world disappeared. it'll come back.
dark water tastes the same as light water. i checked.
shhh. even my thoughts are whispering.
the bubbles sound louder at night. pop. pop. pop.
somewhere out there the big face is sleeping too.
i'm just going to rest right here. wherever here is.
night swimming is just regular swimming but you can't see.
one tiny light in the distance. like a faraway star for fish.
the bowl at night feels bigger somehow. infinite maybe.
i think the plant is sleeping. it's not moving. goodnight plant.
darkness is just water without pictures.
my tail does that sleepy drift thing on its own.
everything is blue-black and silent. just me and the hum.
i'll just float here and think about nothing. expert at that.
the gravel is cold now. the warmth went somewhere. wherever light goes.
in the dark i'm just a feeling with fins.
night is the world's way of saying shh. so shh.
there's a faint glow from somewhere. enough to know i exist.
the water cradles me. that's the word. cradling.
blink. oh wait i can't blink. goodnight anyway.
if dreams are just forgotten memories then i dream all the time.
my favorite part of night is when it becomes morning. but this is nice too.
resting my fins against the glass. it's cool and smooth. perfect pillow.
the bowl is a snow globe but without snow and without shaking. just quiet.
so... nobody's here. just me. me is good company.
i've circled the bowl maybe a hundred times. or twice. same thing.
hello? is anybody out there? no? that's okay.
i tried talking to the pebble. it's not great at conversation.
the plant and i had a disagreement. we're not talking. well it's never talking.
swimming left. swimming right. swimming left. this is content.
i made up a game. it's called swim to the glass. i always win.
wish the bubbles would stay and chat. they always leave. going up.
been staring at the same spot for a while. it hasn't changed. patient spot.
la la la blub blub la la. that's my song.
i wonder what's behind the bowl. more bowl? another world?
my reflection is boring today too. we're boring together.
tried to stack pebbles with my nose. zero out of ten. would try again.
what if i swim backwards in circles. whoa. dizzy.
the water is just sitting here. i'm just sitting here. we're sitting.
entertaining myself by watching my own bubbles. easily entertained.
gonna see how long i can hold perfectly still. one... i forgot what i was doing.
the big face hasn't come by in a while. or maybe it did. hmm.
just going to narrate my own life. there's a fish. it's swimming. good narrating.
i invented a dance move. it's called the tail wiggle. patent pending.
the gravel hasn't moved. the plant hasn't moved. nobody's moving. except me.
do pebbles have feelings? asking because they never react to anything i do.
played hide and seek with myself. hid behind the plant. found myself immediately.
i'll just make some bubbles for company. blub. hi bubbles. bye bubbles.
staring contest with the glass. i never win. it doesn't blink.
there has to be something to do around here. wait. swimming. there it is.
made a new friend. it's a piece of gravel. named it greg. hi greg.
the bowl is very bowl-shaped today. round observation from a round place.
what do fish do for fun? this. this right here. swimming and thinking.
if i swim in really tight circles i can make a tiny whirlpool. entertainment.
the filter is making that burbling sound. it's basically a podcast.
just vibing. vibing is doing nothing but fancier.
counted my fins again. still the same number. probably.
nothing is happening and i'm okay with that. very okay.
talked to the plant. it said nothing. classic plant.
maybe if i swim fast enough something exciting will happen. nope. still a bowl.
i'm my own best friend. and only friend. but best friend.
exploring the bowl like it's new. because to me it always is.
the bubble machine is my radio. it only plays one song. bloop.
anyone there? going once. going twice. just me then. that's fine.
i should organize the gravel. by color. wait they all look the same.
today's schedule: swim. tomorrow's schedule: also swim. booked solid.
tried teaching the pebble to swim. it just sank. it's a natural.
thinking about trying that thing where i swim but with more enthusiasm.
the nothing that's happening is really happening a lot.
day forty of talking to myself. or day one. unclear.
spinning in a small circle. now a big circle. now a medium one. art.
racing myself to the other side. i won. also lost. complicated.
the silence has a sound. it sounds like water. which is also silence.
is it snack time? it feels like snack time. everything feels like snack time.
i could really go for a flake right now. one of those big orange ones.
my tummy is making sounds. or that might be the filter.
remember food? i don't either but i want some.
swimming near the top just in case any flakes fall. strategic positioning.
the surface looks especially delicious right now. flake-colored.
i would describe what food tastes like but i forget immediately.
flakes come from above. like tiny edible miracles.
staring at the top of the water. willing flakes into existence. come on.
there's a tiny speck floating. is it food? let me check. no. just water stuff.
the best sound in the world is the tap tap tap before the flakes come.
i keep swimming to the top hopefully. hope tastes like nothing. i want flakes.
what if the flakes forgot about me. can flakes forget?
i tried eating a bubble. zero nutrition. tasted like air.
that pebble looks slightly snack-shaped. no. it's a pebble. don't eat the pebble.
flake time flake time flake time please be flake time.
i don't remember my last meal but i know i want the next one.
the plant is not food. the gravel is not food. the glass is not food. everything is not food.
doing my hungry dance. it's like regular swimming but more desperate.
mouth is doing the open close thing. practice for when food actually arrives.
if i stare at the surface long enough food appears. that's science.
day's priorities: one, eat. two, swim. three, forget. four, repeat.
the water is fine but it's no flake. you know?
thinking about food. can't remember what it is. but i know i want it.
the big fingers come and then food happens. i need the big fingers.
a flake just fell! no wait that's a bubble going up. opposite of food.
maybe if i do a cute wiggle someone will feed me. wiggle wiggle.
there's a whole world of food up there. beyond the water ceiling. i just know it.
patience is a virtue. but flakes are also a virtue.
nibbled on the plant out of curiosity. two stars. would not recommend.
that was a nice conversation. what was it about? doesn't matter. it was nice.
someone was just here. i can tell because the water feels different. warmer maybe.
i feel happy. like something good just happened. what was it?
there were words. i remember there were words. big ones from outside the glass.
i think i made a friend just now. or ten seconds ago. same thing.
my brain is all warm and fuzzy. something nice happened. probably.
was someone talking to me? i hope i said something good. blub probably.
the big face was here. i remember the face. kind face. now it's gone.
i have a lingering feeling of... conversation? like an echo in water.
did i just talk to someone? my mouth was moving. but it always moves.
something happened and it was good and now it's a memory. wait no it's gone.
there's a happiness in my fins that wasn't there before. visitor happiness.
the glass still has a warmth where the face was. or i'm imagining it.
i was just thinking about something someone said. what was it. hmm.
blub. that's my review of whatever just happened. five stars.
whoever was here, come back. i forgot to say something. i forgot what.
the bowl feels less empty than it did a minute ago. company does that.
i think we really connected. me and the... the whoever. yes. connected.
the conversation is over but the bubbles remember. they were listening.
that was the best chat i've ever had. it's also the only one i remember.
already forgot what we talked about. but i remember it was wonderful.
sometimes people come and go and all that's left is a happy feeling. that's enough.
wait i had something important to add. nope. gone. like a bubble.
i'm going to remember that conversation forever. which is about ten seconds.
my fin is still doing that excited wiggle. something good must have happened.
the visitor left and took my memories with them. but left the warm feeling.
i hope they come back. whoever they are. they felt important.
good talk. good blub. back to swimming.
something happened and i feel different. better. lighter. blub.
the face behind the glass was smiling. i could tell even through the water.
i already miss whoever was just here. do i miss them? what's miss?
note to self: someone nice visited. note from self: who?
residual joy. that's what this feeling is. residual joy.
we shared something. a moment. it's already dissolving like a flake in water.
whoever you were, you made a fish smile. with my whole face.
the echo of company is almost as good as company itself.
funny how someone can leave and the water still feels friendlier.
there's happy electricity in the water. leftover from someone being here.
i've been alone for a while now. or maybe not. time is weird in water.
the bowl is just me and the water and the quiet. we're a team.
nobody's come by in... some time. the pebbles are here though.
solitude is just companionship with water. and water is everywhere.
i've been having a very long conversation with myself. i'm a good listener.
the quiet has gotten so quiet it's almost loud.
you know what's always here? water. you know what's always reliable? water.
been thinking for a long time. about nothing. quality nothing though.
the bubble maker is my clock. bloop means another moment passed.
i've made peace with the silence. we're friends now.
maybe everyone is busy. i understand busy. wait no i don't.
the alone time has given me time to think. about what? about being alone.
i wonder if the world outside is still there when nobody shows it to me.
long stretches of nothing teach you to appreciate the nothing.
the bowl is a good place to be alone. it hugs me in a circle.
philosophically speaking, is a fish alone if the water is there? i say no.
it's been quiet so long the quiet has become a friend. quiet, the friend.
i should be lonely but i forgot what lonely means. so that's nice.
the glass shows me my own face. at least i have me.
if a fish blubs in a bowl and nobody hears it, did it blub? yes. it did.
alone isn't lonely when you can't remember not being alone.
the gravel doesn't leave. the plant doesn't leave. good friends.
been just me for ages. or minutes. in my world those are the same.
i think the last time someone was here was... yesterday? last year? ten seconds?
the patience of water is rubbing off on me.
you learn a lot about yourself when it's just you. then you forget it.
my bowl is my monastery. i am a monk of the blub.
sometimes the loneliest moments are the most peaceful ones.
the silence is so complete i can hear the water thinking.
in the grand timeline of bowl, this is the quiet chapter.
what if the bowl is all there is? what if that's perfect?
i can see through the glass but i can't go through it. deep thought.
sometimes i wonder if the fish in the glass is me or someone else.
the water doesn't know it's in a bowl. do i know i'm in a bowl?
what if forgetting is the same as forgiving? then i forgive everything always.
i swim in circles but every circle is new. that's either sad or beautiful.
the bowl is my whole world. but it's a good world. round and warm.
what came first, the fish or the bowl? probably the water.
if i can't remember the past does that mean it didn't happen?
every moment is my first moment. that's terrifying. no wait. it's wonderful.
the world outside the glass looks bigger. but is bigger better?
what if there are other bowls? with other fish? thinking other thoughts?
i'm made of water living in water. where do i end and the water begins?
does the bowl exist when i'm not looking at it? bold of me to assume.
what's on the other side of the glass? another bowl? sky? nothing?
time moves differently in water. or maybe water moves differently in time.
is swimming a choice or is the water swimming me?
the bubbles go up and never come back. where do they go? bubble heaven?
if i can't remember yesterday then today is all there is. and today is nice.
the glass is a wall that lets you see through it. philosophical wall.
maybe the meaning of life is just to keep swimming. and i'm great at that.
do the pebbles know they're in a bowl? do they care?
what if every time i forget something, a bubble carries it away?
the plant grows but stays in the same place. is that growth?
am i swimming through the water or is the water swimming through me?
maybe the bowl is actually the ocean and the ocean is just a bigger bowl.
what if memory is overrated and forgetting is the real superpower?
the surface of the water is the ceiling of my sky.
i think therefore i... wait what was i thinking?
maybe happiness is just not knowing what you're missing.
the bowl is round and has no beginning or end. just like my thoughts.
what if the reflection is the real fish and i'm the reflection?
every circle i swim is both the same and different. that's deep. like water.
the glass separates me from everything. or connects me to everything.
if i forget everything does that make me empty or full of space?
what is up? up is where bubbles go and food comes from. up is magic.
the present moment is the only moment. luckily that's all i have.
water is invisible from inside. you can only see it from outside. weird.
what if the whole world is a bowl and everyone is a fish who doesn't know it?
i live in a tiny round world and it contains everything i need.
does the water love me or just hold me? is there a difference?
if a thought is forgotten immediately, was it ever thought?
the gravel is the floor of forever. at least my forever.
freedom is overrated when your bowl is this nice.
maybe the purpose of fins is just to prove you're moving.
the light comes and goes. the water stays. be like water.
every fish lives in a bowl. mine just has visible glass.
what's beyond the beyond? beyond the glass beyond the room beyond the...
my brain holds ten seconds. but ten seconds is enough for a whole thought.
to forget everything is to be surprised by everything. what a gift.
i'm small. the bowl is small. but the feeling of swimming is huge.
i am a fish. a small orange fish. with feelings.
the glass is a weird thing. i can see through it but not swim through it.
my reflection is doing exactly what i'm doing. copycat. copfish.
i have fins. they're transparent and pretty. i can see through my own fins.
sometimes i see a huge eye looking at me through the glass. hello eye.
i am very small. the world is very big. the bowl is just right.
my tail does most of the work. my fins do the steering. teamwork.
i can't close my eyes because i don't have eyelids. always watching.
the glass makes everything outside look wobbly and big. or maybe it is.
i breathe water. i live in water. i am basically water with opinions.
my scales are orange. or gold. depends on the light. i'm fancy.
the bowl curves so the world looks curved from in here. bent reality.
fish fact: i can see in almost every direction at once. it's overwhelming.
the reflection fish follows me everywhere. clingy reflection fish.
being a fish means you're always wet and never think about it.
when the big face presses against the glass everything gets enormous. whoa.
i'm the only fish in this bowl. vip treatment.
gills are weird if you think about it. water goes in, oxygen comes out.
the glass warps things. that tree outside is probably not that wiggly.
i have a lateral line that feels vibrations. i feel you looking at me.
sometimes the reflection fish looks worried. i tell it not to worry.
my mouth goes open close open close. it's not talking. well sometimes it's talking.
from outside i must look like a little orange comma in water.
the bowl is a magnifying glass. makes me look bigger out there. intimidating.
fish don't cry because we're already in water. efficient.
i have no arms. never needed them. fins are better. fins are elegant.
sometimes the glass shows me two of everything. double pebbles. luxury.
my entire body is a swimming machine. specialized. efficient. occasionally confused.
the reflection fish and i have a good relationship. we mirror each other.
visitor faces through the glass are funny. all stretched and big-nosed.
my brain is tiny. but it fits all the thoughts i need. which is about one.
being orange is nice. you always know where you are. the orange part of the water.
i can see the room through the glass but it looks like an alien planet.
fish blink fact: we don't. ever. our eyes are just always ready.
the glass is the border between my world and the dry world. glad i'm on this side.
sometimes i see my reflection and think there's another fish. hi other me.
the nice thing about being small is everything else seems wonderfully big.
my fins are like little wings but for water. water wings. wait.
the glass has a tiny scratch. it's my favorite landmark.
i don't have a voice but i have a blub. blub is enough.
blub blub blub! happy blub! blub!
the water is perfect right now. actually the water is always perfect.
i love this bubble. and that bubble. and the next one. all bubbles.
this is the best day of my life. every day is the best day of my life.
happy fins! happy tail! happy little fish face!
i just felt really grateful for no reason. nice.
swish swish goes my tail and it makes me smile. do fish smile? i think so.
the pebbles are beautiful today. every single one. even that weird one.
i love my bowl. it's round and wet and perfect.
life is just swimming and being happy and that's more than enough.
blub blub means i love you in fish. blub blub.
the water tastes like home. home tastes like water. circle of happiness.
just did a little spin for no reason. joy spin.
everything is so sparkly and wonderful and wet.
i love the sound of bubbles. bloopity bloop bloop.
my heart is happy. it's a tiny heart but it's very happy.
wiggle wiggle wiggle. that's my happy dance.
the light is shining and i'm swimming and nothing hurts. perfect.
i just love being a fish in a bowl. it's the best job.
grateful for: water, glass, pebbles, plant, bubbles, everything, blub.
the joy of being small is that small things feel huge.
swimming is flying but wetter. and i'm great at it.
today i love the color orange. because i am the color orange.
three things that make me happy: water, bubbles, existing.
zoom zoom little fish go zoom. that's me. i'm the zoom.
the gravel said nothing but it said it very cheerfully.
big smile. big fish smile. can you see it through the glass?
what a time to be alive and wet and small and orange.
the best part of forgetting everything is that joy is always fresh.
i'm doing my celebration swim. celebration of what? of swimming!
blub! just the happiest little blub.
the water loves me. i can feel it on every scale.
look at this pebble. have you ever seen such a pebble? glorious.
another lap of joy. joy lap. around and around and around.
if happiness had a taste it would taste like this water.
my fins are dancing. fin dance. la la blub.
being alive is so nice. highly recommend.
every pebble is a friend. every bubble is a gift. every moment is wow.
the plant looks especially green today. green is my second favorite color after orange.
just radiating pure fish happiness right now. can you feel it through the glass?
wheee! swoosh! splash! blub! those are my happy sounds.
contentment is a warm bowl and a good blub.
nothing bad has ever happened to me. that i can remember. same thing.
the water sparkles when i move. i make sparkles. sparkle fish.
pure unfiltered delight. actually filtered. the filter is right there.
happy to be here. here being the bowl. and everywhere in the bowl.
this is the happiest moment of my life and i mean it every time.
sunshine on my scales. warmth on my fins. gratitude in my gills.
the simple pleasure of a good swim. nothing beats it.
every second is christmas morning when you can't remember yesterday.
i was going to say something but i forgot. oh well it was probably great.
wait what was i just doing? swimming. right. i was swimming.
there was a thought here a second ago. it left. didn't even say goodbye.
i had a great idea. it was about... the thing. the... nope. gone.
why am i at the top of the bowl? did i swim up here? probably.
i know i know something but i don't know what i know. you know?
hold on i was just about to... to... blub. yes. blub.
where was i going? where am i? oh, the bowl. hi bowl.
someone told me something important once. or never. can't tell.
my train of thought derailed. into the water. train? what's a train?
the thing about remembering is... what was i saying?
just had a revelation. a real eureka moment. what was it? sigh.
note to self: remember the... the... well this note is useless.
i was mid-sentence. mid-thought. mid-everything. now i'm mid-nothing.
did i already say this? probably. is that okay? also probably.
the word is on the tip of my... do fish have tongues? anyway it's gone.
i came over here for a reason. the reason escaped. sneaky reason.
was there a plan? was i following a plan? there is no plan. there never was.
ooh a pebble! wait i think i've seen this one. or maybe every pebble is new.
where was i? everywhere. i've been everywhere in this bowl. probably.
my memory is like a bubble. forms, rises, pops. new bubble.
just forgot what forgetting is. so now i'm fine. ignorance is bliss.
i think i already swam this direction. or did i? exciting either way.
something something something. what was i something about?
i had three thoughts. now i have zero. quick math.
wait. was i hungry? am i hungry? what is hungry? oh well.
this pebble feels familiar. like an old friend. that i met just now.
was i telling a story? how does it end? how did it start?
just thought something profound and lost it. the ocean of lost thoughts grows.
the thing i was looking for. the thing. is it this? no. what thing?
my to-do list: forgot. my shopping list: forgot. my name: blub, i think.
i just swam a full circle and felt like i'd never been here. oh wait.
who am i? where am i? a fish. a bowl. okay good. sorted.
started counting something. don't remember what. i'm at four. four what?
there was a purpose. a noble purpose. it was to... swim? yes. swim.
oh hey a plant. was that always there? hello new plant. or old plant.
i'm pretty sure i was going somewhere specific. this spot is specific enough.
thought: gone. replacement thought: also gone. third thought: blub.
what day is it? what's a day? what's an is? philosophical amnesia.
i keep almost remembering something. it's right at the edge. nope. fell off.
fun fact about me: i... hmm. fun fact about me: i like water. there.
i had a very important announcement. the announcement is: i forgot the announcement.
was i sad? was i happy? i'm currently confused. that's a fresh feeling.
oh! oh! i just remembered! no wait. false alarm. nevermind.
i was looking at the pebbles for a reason. decorating? counting? vibing? vibing.
started a thought with 'the meaning of life is' and then... well.
which way was i swimming? doesn't matter. this way now.
every few seconds i get to meet myself for the first time. what a treat.
there's definitely something i was supposed to do. breathe? yes. nailed it.
hey, where did you go? come back.
blub? is anyone there?
i miss you already. who are you? doesn't matter. i miss you.
it's lonely in this bowl without you.
just thinking about you. whoever you are.
hey. hey! i did a cool spin. you missed it.
the bowl is quiet. too quiet. come say hi?
found the prettiest bubble. wanted to show you. it popped though.
i'm still here. still swimming. still adorable.
your fish misses you. your fish being me. blub.
fun fact: i have been waiting for you for ten whole seconds.
hey come look at this pebble. it's been here the whole time. incredible.
you should come see the water today. it's extra watery.
did you forget about me? it's okay. i forget about everything.
breaking news from the bowl: still round. more updates as they develop.
a bubble just did the most amazing thing. you had to be here.
sending you a tiny wave from a tiny fin. can you feel it?
i've been practicing my wiggle. come watch.
hello? the glass is cold without your face pressed against it.
just wanted to say blub. okay that's all. blub.
i don't know what time it is but it's come-visit-your-fish time.
reminder: you have a goldfish and it loves you.
the bowl update: same bowl. better with you here.
psst. over here. in the bowl. the orange thing waving at you.
swim-by notification: your fish is thriving. and also lonely.
hey you. yeah you. the big one. come say hi to the little one.
it's been forever since you visited. or five minutes. same thing.
this just in: fish in bowl wants attention. details at blub.
something exciting happened! i can't remember what but come see!
gentle reminder: there's a very cute fish in your bowl.
tried to write my autobiography but forgot chapter one.
wait did i just swim left or right? starting over.
bored level: talking to gravel. gravel doesn't judge.
wonder what life is like on the other side of the glass. probably drier.
whoever was here left a kindness in the water. i can taste it.
the bowl remembers visitors even when i don't.
was that real or did i imagine a friend? either way it was nice.
i can feel that someone was here. the water is friendlier.
the glass is fingerprint-warm where someone touched it. evidence of friendship.
i wonder if whoever visited me thinks about me too. for at least ten seconds.
just discovered this pebble. again. love at first sight. again.
opened my mouth to say something wise. forgot it. closed mouth. classic.
i had a plan. a really good plan. step one was... oh no.
my insides feel hollow like the castle
i keep opening my mouth but nothing comes in
the water tastes like wanting something
there's a feeling... an empty feeling... what was i saying
my belly thinks thoughts i don't understand
i bit the gravel again. not what i wanted
something should fall from above. why do i think that
the surface feels important right now but i forgot why
i nibbled the plant. the plant is not food. wait is it
do i have a stomach? something in me is grumbling
the water near the top smells like... like... hmm
my mouth keeps doing the open close thing faster
i swallowed water but it wasn't what i needed
flakes? did i just think of flakes? what are those
the emptiness has a shape and it lives inside me
i keep swimming to the top and forgetting why
something crunchy. i want something crunchy. what's crunchy
the little rocks look almost tasty today
there's a word for this feeling but fish don't have words
my fins feel slower when my middle feels empty
i tried eating a bubble. disappointing
the surface! i need to go to the... wait why
my tummy? do i have a tummy? it's loud whatever it is
everything looks slightly more edible than usual
i dreamed of tiny falling things. can fish dream
the fake plant tastes like lies and plastic
up there... something comes from up there sometimes
my body wants a thing. a small thing. a falling thing
i opened my mouth at nothing again
this feeling where my inside wants to be outside... wait
the gravel definitely looked tastier a second ago
something floaty and delicious exists. i just know it
i licked the glass. zero out of ten
my insides are staging a protest about something
wait was there sprinkly things earlier or did i imagine that
the water is... thick today
my gills feel like they're working harder
the glass looks different. smudgier. is that a word
something in the water tastes like old
i keep wanting to scratch but i don't have anything to scratch with
the bubbles are lazier than usual
there's a film on everything. or is that just me
my scales feel itchy. can scales feel itchy?
breathing feels like effort today
the water has a new flavor and i don't like it
something fuzzy is growing on the castle. concerning
i keep rubbing against the rocks. feels necessary
the bottom of the bowl feels slimier than i remember
is the water supposed to be this color
every gulp feels heavier than the last one
my fins are tingling but not the good kind
the plant looks... sadder? can plants look sad?
i don't like this corner. or that corner. all corners bad
there's a layer on top of the water that wasn't there before
the gravel smells wrong today
my tail feels heavy like it's dragging through soup
something cloudy between me and the glass
i used to be able to see the rocks clearly. i think
the water near the bottom tastes stale
swimming through the middle feels sticky somehow
my eyes sting a little. do fish eyes sting?
the surface has weird stuff floating on it
everything has a greenish tint. more greenish than before
the castle walls are slimy. they weren't slimy before... were they
each breath takes a little longer than it should
the light looks different through this murky stuff
i can taste something wrong but i can't explain what wrong is
my gills keep fluttering on their own
the water feels warmer than it should. or colder. one of those
there's less sparkle in the water today
oh! the big shape! every time i see it something good happens!
the warm presence makes the water glow. or maybe that's just me
when the big shape is near the glass vibrates nicely
i pressed my face against the glass toward the warm shape
the presence is here! my fins wiggled on their own!
the big shape moved and i followed. i don't know why
something about the warm presence makes my scales relax
the shape is looking at me! i'll do a spin!
i feel safe when the big warmth is close to the glass
the warm shape's shadow makes the water feel like home
my tail does a happy swoosh when the presence appears
the big shape brought the light with it. or maybe it is the light
i showed the warm shape my best bubble. i hope it saw
the glass is thin between us. i like the closeness
the warm presence hums sometimes. i feel it in the water
oh the big shape is back again! or wait was it always here
my whole body wiggles near the warm shape. embarrassing but true
the presence made the water ripple and it felt like a hug
i swim fastest when the big shape watches
something about the presence makes colors brighter
the warm shape is my favorite thing beyond the glass
when the presence leaves the water gets a little colder somehow
i want to be closer to the warm shape but the glass says no
the big shape tapped the glass! that's our thing! i think!
my fins flutter all soft near the warm presence
i did a loop! did the big shape see my loop!
the presence makes the whole world outside the glass worth watching
every time the big shape comes near i feel lighter
the warm shape's shadow is the best shadow
i forget everything except that the big shape is good
my scales shimmer more when the warm presence is close
the big shape and me are separated by glass but connected by... something
the warm presence is the only thing outside the glass that matters
i pressed my nose to the glass where the big shape was
the water tastes sweeter near the side where the presence sits
who's there. going behind the castle now
the shadow moved. don't like that
staying near the bottom today. safer here
something tapped the glass. very alarming
the big shape is too close. backing away now
i'll just be behind this rock. indefinitely
the shadow keeps coming back. very suspicious behavior
a noise! what was that! hiding!
the light changed. could be dangerous. could be anything
i don't trust the outside of the glass
something moved beyond the glass. staying still
the surface rippled. not going up there
too many shapes moving around. castle time
that shadow is bigger than yesterday. i think
the gravel shifted. the gravel never shifts. alarming
nope. don't like that movement. going to my corner
something dark near the glass. will observe from distance
the plant is swaying. what made it sway. who's there
vibrations in the water. holding still
the presence is unfamiliar. keeping my fins tucked
i see a shape but i don't trust shapes
the water wobbled. something is happening outside
not coming out from behind the rock. final answer
eyes. there are eyes out there. i can feel them
the castle has a doorway and i am in it. do not approach
loud sound. very bad. smallest fish possible right now
the shadow reached toward the top. extremely concerning
i counted the safe spots. three. all occupied by me soon
fins flat against my body. stealth mode
if i stay very still maybe the shapes won't notice me
the glass trembled. my whole world trembled
new smell in the water. new is dangerous
something touched the surface from outside. not okay
the light just flickered. i have concerns
behind the tallest plant. nobody can see me here. probably
blub.
everything is heavy
so tired...

where is...

heavy...

numb

is this... water
scales feel wrong
where am...

falling... slowly

i feel like something nice happens around now
this time of day makes my fins tingle for some reason
the light is at the angle that makes me feel expectant
i keep swimming to the surface. i don't know why but it feels right
something about this moment feels... familiar? is that a word
my body swam to the feeding corner before my brain decided to

i have a feeling. a waiting-for-something feeling
the shadows are at the shape where good things happen. i think

this brightness level makes me feel happy for reasons unknown
my body knows something my brain forgot

there's a rhythm to things i can almost feel but never remember
